heading: Exclusion from United States of aliens who have confiscated property of United States nationals or who traffic in such property
label: 6091 Exclusion from United States of aliens who have confiscated property of United States nationals or who traffic in such property
notes: References in TextSubchapter III, referred to in subsec. Reports on Determinations Under Title IV of the LIBERTAD Act, subdiv. B, title XXVIII, § 2802, , , as amended by , , , 1501A–423; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–277, div. G112 Stat. 2681–845Pub. L. 106–113, div. B, § 1000(a)(7) [div. A, title II, § 209(b)]113 Stat. 1536Pub. L. 107–228, div. A, title II, § 216(b)116 Stat. 1366Oct. 21, 1998Nov. 29, 1999Sept. 30, 2002“(a).—Reports RequiredNot later than 30 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [] and every 3 months thereafter during the period ending , the Secretary of State shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees [Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives and Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate] a report on the implementation of section 401 of the Cuban Liberty and Democratic Solidarity (LIBERTAD) Act of 1996 (). Each report shall include—Oct. 21, 1998September 30, 200322 U.S.C. 6091“(1)an unclassified list, by economic sector, of the number of entities then under review pursuant to that section;“(2)an unclassified list of all entities and a classified list of all individuals that the Secretary of State has determined to be subject to that section;“(3)an unclassified list of all entities and a classified list of all individuals that the Secretary of State has determined are no longer subject to that section;“(4)an explanation of the status of the review underway for the cases referred to in paragraph (1); and“(5)an unclassified explanation of each determination of the Secretary of State under section 401(a) of that Act and each finding of the Secretary under section 401(c) of that Act—“(A)since the date of the enactment of this Act, in the case of the first report under this subsection; and“(B)in the preceding 3-month period, in the case of each subsequent report.“(b).—Protection of Identity of Concerned EntitiesIn preparing the report under subsection (a), the names of entities shall not be identified under paragraph (1) or (4).”

children.text: has confiscated, or has directed or overseen the confiscation of, property a claim to which is owned by a United States national, or converts or has converted for personal gain confiscated property, a claim to which is owned by a United States national;
traffics in confiscated property, a claim to which is owned by a United States national;

is a corporate officer, principal, or shareholder with a controlling interest of an entity which has been involved in the confiscation of property or trafficking in confiscated property, a claim to which is owned by a United States national; or
is a spouse, minor child, or agent of a person excludable under paragraph (1), (2), or (3).

children.label: i
children.text: without the property having been returned or adequate and effective compensation provided; or

children.label: ii
children.text: without the claim to the property having been settled pursuant to an international claims settlement agreement or other mutually accepted settlement procedure; and

children.label: i
children.text: a debt of any enterprise which has been nationalized, expropriated, or otherwise taken by the Cuban Government;

children.label: ii
children.text: a debt which is a charge on property nationalized, expropriated, or otherwise taken by the Cuban Government; or

children.label: iii
children.text: a debt which was incurred by the Cuban Government in satisfaction or settlement of a confiscated property claim.

children.label: I
children.text: transfers, distributes, dispenses, brokers, or otherwise disposes of confiscated property,

children.label: II
children.text: purchases, receives, obtains control of, or otherwise acquires confiscated property, or

children.label: III
children.text: improves (other than for routine maintenance), invests in (by contribution of funds or anything of value, other than for routine maintenance), or begins after , to manage, lease, possess, use, or hold an interest in confiscated property,March 12, 1996

children.label: ii
children.text: enters into a commercial arrangement using or otherwise benefiting from confiscated property, or

children.label: iii
children.text: causes, directs, participates in, or profits from, trafficking (as described in clause (i) or (ii)) by another person, or otherwise engages in trafficking (as described in clause (i) or (ii)) through another person,

children.label: i
children.text: the delivery of international telecommunication signals to Cuba;

children.label: ii
children.text: the trading or holding of securities publicly traded or held, unless the trading is with or by a person determined by the Secretary of the Treasury to be a specially designated national;

children.label: iii
children.text: transactions and uses of property incident to lawful travel to Cuba, to the extent that such transactions and uses of property are necessary to the conduct of such travel; or

children.label: iv
children.text: transactions and uses of property by a person who is both a citizen of Cuba and a resident of Cuba, and who is not an official of the Cuban Government or the ruling political party in Cuba.

children.heading: Exemption
children.label: c Exemption
children.text: This section shall not apply where the Secretary of State finds, on a case by case basis, that the entry into the United States of the person who would otherwise be excluded under this section is necessary for medical reasons or for purposes of litigation of an action under subchapter III.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: This section applies to aliens seeking to enter the United States on or after .March 12, 1996

children.heading: Trafficking
children.label: 2 Trafficking
children.text: This section applies only with respect to acts within the meaning of “traffics” that occur on or after .March 12, 1996
